theory.canonical_statement: systems; underpins quantum numbers; connects to flux quantization. Falsifiability / Test Handle: Detection of noninteger charges or spin values not explained by composite structures would falsify this entry. 2.0.34 ID 183: Emergent Gravity from Coherence Restoration Type: Structural Constraint Formal Statement: Gravity arises as a collective tendency of topological defects to restore coherence; summing over a mass distribution $ρ(r)\rho(\mathbf{r})ρ(r)$ yields a potential $Φ\PhiΦ$ satisfying $∇2Φ=4πGeffρ\nablaˆ2$ \Phi = 4\pi G_{\text{eff}} $\rho∇2Φ=4πGeffρ,$ where GeffG_{\text{eff}}Geff is derived from the vacuum’s restoring tendency. Interpretive Meaning: Particles, as defects, create gradients in the coherence field; these gradients produce an attractive potential that can be formalized by Poisson’s equation; gravity is an emergent entropic force rather than a fundamental interaction. Dependencies: ID
